title: Rich Uruguayan Ikura Gratin
description: A rich, crumbly weeknight Uruguayan dish featuring ikura, bonito shaved into thin strips and low in carbs.

ingredients.name: Ikura
ingredients.notes: Fresh ikura with the eggs still intact

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, gently combine the ikura, bonito flakes, sour cream, and grated Parmesan cheese.
Transfer the mixture to a 6-inch (15 cm) baking dish and smooth the top.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the filling is heated through.
Remove the gratin from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top and season with freshly ground black pepper.
Serve the rich Uruguayan Ikura Gratin warm, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Note: Traditionally, the ikura would be cooked in a cast-iron skillet over low heat, but for convenience, baking is recommended for a richer, creamier flavor.
This recipe serves 1 as a main course, 2 as a side dish, or 4 as an appetizer.
The total cooking time can be adjusted based on the size of the baking dish and the number of servings.
This dish is perfect for a weeknight dinner or a special occasion meal.
It can be made ahead and refrigerated for up to 24 hours or frozen for up to 2 months.
